title: Fix label dimensions, barcode width, and scanning validation

requirements.summary: Update label dimensions to 58mm × 43mm and ensure all components use these exact dimensions
acceptanceCriteria: Label settings store default dimensions are set to 58mm × 43mm
CPCL generator uses 58mm × 43mm for label boundaries

file_operations.description: Update the default label dimensions in the store initialization from current values to widthMm: 58 and heightMm: 43. Ensure these defaults apply to new installations and are used when no persisted settings exist.

Verify that CPCL generation respects the widthMm and heightMm values from labelSettings for label boundaries. Ensure all positioning and clipping logic uses these dimensions (converted to dots at 203 DPI) rather than hardcoded values.

Ensure the preview component renders labels using the exact dimensions from labelSettings (58mm × 43mm). Verify the preview canvas scales correctly to display the full label area at various zoom levels.

requirements.summary: Adjust barcode width parameters to ensure both barcodes fit completely within the 58mm label width for scannable output
acceptanceCriteria: Both barcodes fit within 58mm label width with no clipping
Barcode width is adjusted in CPCL generation to maximize width while staying within boundaries

Generated labels match the visual layout of the reference photos (frontend/public/assets/1771682557066499855940811118309.jpg, frontend/public/assets/image-11.png, frontend/public/assets/17717694923414593234117505414352.jpg, frontend/public/assets/image-12.png)
Printed barcodes are scannable without manual adjustment

file_operations.description: Update the barcode width calculation logic to ensure barcodes span nearly the full 58mm label width (converted to dots) while avoiding clipping. Adjust the clampBarcodeX function and estimateCODE128WidthDots to account for the new label dimensions and match the reference photo layout where barcodes extend edge-to-edge.

Adjust the CPCL barcode generation commands to use optimized width parameters that maximize barcode width within the 58mm label boundaries. Ensure both barcode1 and barcode2 use consistent width settings and apply layout adjustments from cpclLayoutAdjustments.ts. Add validation logging to confirm barcodes fit within label bounds.

Update the preview rendering to reflect the adjusted barcode widths. Ensure the preview accurately shows barcodes spanning nearly the full label width, matching the reference photos. Apply the same width calculations used in CPCL generation to the SVG preview elements.

Review and adjust default barcode layout settings (barcode1Layout.width, barcode2Layout.width) to support wider barcodes within the 58mm label. Ensure these defaults produce scannable barcodes that match the reference photo layout.

requirements.summary: When scanning with incorrect barcode prefix, display error, play sound, and automatically clear input for immediate re-scan
acceptanceCriteria: Error message displays when scanned barcode prefix does not match any configured prefix mapping
Error sound plays when incorrect prefix detected
Input box is cleared automatically after showing error
User can immediately scan again without manual intervention
Validation applies to both first and second barcode scans

file_operations.description: Enhance the barcode scanning validation logic for both serial1 and serial2 inputs. When a scanned barcode prefix does not match any configured prefix mapping in labelSettings, trigger the error sound from soundSystem, display a clear error message indicating the invalid prefix, and automatically clear the input field (set value to empty string) so the user can scan again immediately. Ensure this validation runs before any other processing and applies to both first and second barcode scans.

requirements.summary: Detect and show an error if the user scans the same serial number again (duplicate serial detection)
acceptanceCriteria: System detects when the same serial number is scanned twice
Error message displays clearly indicating duplicate serial
Duplicate serial is not processed or printed
Error sound plays on duplicate detection

file_operations.description: Add duplicate serial detection logic to the scanning workflow. Track recently scanned serials (consider using a Set or array of recent scans, possibly stored in component state or a ref). When a serial is scanned, check if it matches any recently scanned serial. If a duplicate is detected, display a clear error message (e.g., 'Duplicate serial detected'), play the error sound from soundSystem, and prevent further processing or printing. Clear the input field automatically. Consider defining a reasonable time window or count for duplicate detection (e.g., last 10 scans or scans within the last session).
